title: Monday Formulas - the SECRET Functions

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Everybody,

As many of you are aware, I have been doing a series of videos on the monday formula column. Today I posted the 4th. In it, I show a few of the (over 300) undocumented functions available to use.

Two words: holidays & regular expressions!

In the video description is a formula that will allow you to subtract workdays from a date including holidays .

There is also a link to the documentation, that I started, giving details on these “secret” functions.

If you like monday formulas, you might want to check it out:

Jim - Subscribe to The Monday Man
Watch Our Latest Video: monday Formulas: the SECRET Functions

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Halcyon

You and me both… A lot of work went into this one!

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Amazing @JCorrell. These videos are so incredibly helpful to all!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mark.anley

Thank you.

I wish I knew about the regular expressions earlier… I use them all the time now.

At least everyone now has a place to go to find out about these undocumented functions.

Jim - Subscribe to The Monday Man
Watch Our Latest Video: monday Formulas: the SECRET Functions

posts.author: JoseDaher (Jose Daher)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! I love your work, but I was trying to reach the hidden functions list in your site, but… Now it’s all new, it’s very nice and looks great, but… Where is the complete list? Is there any place to find it?
